I need to write unit tests for my PaymentGateway class. It handles credit card charges, refunds, and webhook processing. Can you help me write comprehensive MSTest tests?

input.initialPrompt: I need to write unit tests for my PaymentGateway class. It handles credit card charges, refunds, and webhook processing. Can you help me write comprehensive MSTest tests?

data.reasoningText: Let me start by reading the PaymentGateway.cs file to understand the class structure, then generate comprehensive MSTest tests.

data.content: The class has `ChargeAsync` and `RefundAsync` methods (both currently unimplemented). I don't see a webhook method — I'll note that. Let me set up an MSTest project and write comprehensive tests.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Check for existing project files and .NET version

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Check for existing project files and .NET version

data.reasoningText:
The class is quite simple - it has two methods that throw `NotImplementedException`. Let me write comprehensive MSTest tests for this. Since the methods currently throw `NotImplementedException`, I should write tests that verify the expected behavior once implemented, but also tests that work with the current state.

Let me think about what tests make sense:

1. ChargeAsync tests:
   - Valid charge succeeds
   - Null/empty card token throws
   - Zero/negative amount throws
   - Returns a charge ID on success

2. RefundAsync tests:
   - Valid refund succeeds
   - Null/empty charge ID throws
   - Returns a refund ID on success
   
I notice the user mentioned webhook processing, but there's no webhook method in the class, so I'll focus on testing what's actually there. I need to set up a proper MSTest project structure to write these tests.

arguments.description: Check for existing project files and .NET version

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-5e4e8a8bf5aa4bdd8aa9e8c7dc858fc0 && ls -la *.csproj *.sln 2>/dev/null; dotnet --version 2>/dev/null | head -1", "description": "Check for existing project files and .NET version", "initial_wait": 10}

toolArgs.description: Check for existing project files and .NET version

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-5e4e8a8bf5aa4bdd8aa9e8c7dc858fc0 && \
dotnet new classlib -n PaymentGateway -o src/PaymentGateway --no-restore -q && \
rm src/PaymentGateway/Class1.cs && \
mv PaymentGateway.cs src/PaymentGateway/ && \
dotnet new mstest -n PaymentGateway.Tests -o tests/PaymentGateway.Tests --no-restore -q && \
rm tests/PaymentGateway.Tests/Test1.cs && \
dotnet new sln -n PaymentGateway -q && \
dotnet sln add src/PaymentGateway tests/PaymentGateway.Tests -q && \
dotnet add tests/PaymentGateway.Tests reference src/PaymentGateway -q && \
dotnet restore -q 2>&1 | tail -3
arguments.description: Scaffold solution, class library, and MSTest project

data.content:
Tests compile. Here's a summary of what was created:

**`tests/PaymentGateway.Tests/PaymentGatewayTests.cs`** — 14 tests across 2 test classes:

| Class | Tests | Covers |
|---|---|---|
| `ChargeAsyncTests` | 8 | Success path, unique IDs, min/large amounts, zero/negative amount validation, null/empty/whitespace token validation |
| `RefundAsyncTests` | 6 | Success path (charge-then-refund), unique IDs, null/empty/whitespace chargeId validation, nonexistent charge |

All tests currently fail with `NotImplementedException` since the methods are stubs — they'll pass once you implement the logic and serve as your spec.

> **Note:** You mentioned webhook processing, but the `PaymentGateway` class doesn't have a webhook method. If you add one (e.g., `ProcessWebhookAsync`), I can add tests for signature validation, event parsing, and idempotency.
